59.reason: The condition should be strcmp(w, p->word) < 0 to break when the word should be inserted before p, not when they are different. This causes incorrect insertion order and duplicates.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  The condition should be strcmp(w, p->word) < 0 to break when the word should be inserted before p, not when they are different. This causes incorrect insertion order and duplicates.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The condition should be strcmp(w, p->word) < 0 to break when the word is less than the current node's word, ensuring lexicographic order. Using != 0 breaks incorrectly for any mismatch.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  The condition should be 'strcmp(w, p->word) < 0' to correctly find the insertion point when the list is sorted. The current condition breaks on any mismatch, causing the loop to stop prematurely and preventing proper duplicate detection and sorted order.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Condition should be <0 to break for sorted insertion; !=0 will break on any mismatch, preventing traversal.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The condition to break the loop is incorrect. It should break when the current word in the list is lexicographically greater than the input word (strcmp > 0), not just when they are different. This prevents correct insertion in sorted order.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Logic error: The loop breaks on the first non-matching word instead of finding the correct sorted position. It should break if w < p->word to maintain lexicographical order]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect condition: breaks on first non-matching word even if correct position hasn't been found; should check if w < p->word to break for insertion order]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect logic: breaks on first non-matching word even if target word should appear later; should check if w < p->word to break for insertion point in sorted list]
